This repository is designed so AI models (and human operators) can read the codebase, implement changes confidently, and ship to QA/Production via Git—zero manual deployments.

> **Philosophy:** 100% Cloud-Native · AI-Driven · Git-Only

## 1. Overview

**{{PROJECT\_DISPLAY\_NAME}}** is a generic, production-grade application foundation for cloud-native **web applications** (sites, backends, internal tools, and more). It ships with a pre-configured secure backend and a ready-to-use integration with **Google Vertex AI (Gemini)** so you can add generative AI features from day one—without wrestling with infrastructure.

This template is generated and managed by **WIZBI** and embraces an **AI-first workflow**: development tasks are defined in Git, an operator prompts an AI assistant to read the repo and produce code changes, and a simple **git push** triggers automated deployments.

---

## 2. Core Architecture & Features

**Serverless & Scalable**

* Lightweight **Node.js/Express** backend running on a **private Google Cloud Run** service that auto-scales with demand.

**Secure by Design**

* **Firebase Hosting** acts as the secure gateway and global CDN.
* The backend service is **not publicly exposed**; Firebase invokes it privately using **IAM**.

**AI-Ready Backend**

* Fully functional, streaming **`/api/chat`** endpoint wired to **Vertex AI (Gemini)** for easy, reliable AI integration.

**Automated CI/CD**

* **GitHub Actions** pipeline handles test, build, and deploy to **QA** and **Production** automatically.

**High-Level Flow**

```
User
  ↓
Firebase Hosting (CDN)
  ↓  [Rewrite Rule]
[SECURE IAM INVOCATION]
  ↓
Private Cloud Run Service
  ↓
Vertex AI (Gemini)
```

---

## 3. Dev & Deployment Workflow (CI/CD)

> ⚠️ **Do not deploy manually.** The **git push** is the only deployment trigger.

1. **Task Definition** – Describe a feature/bug (e.g., “Create a new API endpoint at `/api/inventory`”).
2. **Code Generation** – An operator prompts an AI assistant to read the repository and generate the full set of code changes.
3. **Commit** – Validate locally and commit to a branch.
4. **Deployment Trigger** – Pushing the branch runs the CI/CD pipeline.
5. **Push to `dev`** – Automatically deploys to **QA**.
6. **Merge to `main`** – Automatically deploys to **Production**.
7. **Verification** – Track progress in the GitHub **Actions** tab and test the app at its environment URL.

---

## 4. Working with AI Models (Agent-Oriented Development)

This repository is intentionally structured so AI agents can understand it quickly and make safe edits.

**Agent-Friendly Conventions**

* **Clear file roles:** Routes in `src/routes/*`, services in `src/services/*`, entrypoint in `src/index.ts`.
* **Stable contracts:** Public API endpoints and service boundaries are explicit and documented in code comments.
* **Guardrails:** Some files are **off-limits** (see Notes) to keep CI/CD and infra stable.
* **Small, composable modules:** Encourages minimal, reviewable diffs that are easy to test.

**Suggested Prompt Template (for Operators)**

```
You are an expert TypeScript/Express engineer working in {{PROJECT_DISPLAY_NAME}}.
Goal: <clear description of the feature/bugfix>

Constraints:
- Do not change .github/workflows/deploy.yml (managed by WIZBI).
- Keep TypeScript strictness and existing lint rules.
- Place HTTP routes under src/routes/, shared logic under src/services/.
- Update/extend tests if present; ensure build passes.

Acceptance:
- New/changed endpoints documented in code comments (JSDoc).
- No breaking changes to existing public APIs unless specified.

Deliverables:
- The complete, ready-to-commit content of all modified/new files.
- A short test plan and any required environment variable notes.
```

**Commit Message Template**

```
feat(api): add /api/inventory with validation and service layer

- Implement route in src/routes/inventory.ts
- Add business logic in src/services/inventory.ts
- Extend Vertex AI service usage where needed
- Update index.ts wiring and docs

Test plan:
- curl POST /api/inventory ... -> 200
- Unit tests pass locally
```

---

## 5. Built-in AI: `/api/chat` Endpoint

A streaming chat endpoint connected to **Vertex AI (Gemini)**. Use it from any frontend or service.

### Request (POST `/api/chat`)

```json
{
  "message": "What are the top 5 benefits of using a serverless architecture?",
  "history": [
    { "role": "user",  "parts": [{ "text": "Hi, can you explain cloud computing?" }] },
    { "role": "model", "parts": [{ "text": "Of course! Cloud computing is..." }] }
  ]
}
```

### Example `curl`

```bash
curl -X POST https://<your-domain>/api/chat \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "message": "What are the top 5 benefits of using a serverless architecture?",
    "history": [
      { "role": "user",  "parts": [{ "text": "Hi, can you explain cloud computing?" }] },
      { "role": "model", "parts": [{ "text": "Of course! Cloud computing is..." }] }
    ]
  }'
```

> **Streaming:** The response is streamed token-by-token. Your frontend should read the stream and render incrementally.

---

## 6. Key Files & Directories

* `src/index.ts` — Backend entrypoint. Register routes and common middleware here.
* `src/services/vertex.ts` — Core integration with **Vertex AI**.
* `src/routes/ai.ts` — Defines the **`/api/chat`** endpoint (add more AI-related routes here).
* `public/` — Static frontend assets (HTML/CSS/JS). Default `index.html` is a placeholder.
* `package.json` — Dependencies and scripts (Express, Vertex AI SDK, etc.).
* `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` — CI/CD pipeline (managed by **WIZBI**; **do not modify**).
* `Dockerfile` — Backend container definition.
* `firebase.json` — Firebase Hosting config and rewrite rules to the private backend service.

## 8. Local Run (Optional)

Local runs are for development only; cloud deploys are managed by WIZBI.

```bash
# Install dependencies
npm install

# Start (depends on scripts in package.json)
npm run dev    # or: npm start
```

---

## 9. Important Notes

* 🚫 **Do not modify** `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` — it is fully managed by **WIZBI**.
* 🚀 **Deployments are Git-driven only**:

  * Push to `dev` ⇒ deploys to **QA**
  * Merge to `main` ⇒ deploys to **Production**
* 🔐 The Cloud Run service is **private** and invoked only via Firebase + IAM. Do **not** expose it publicly.
* 🤖 When adding features, prefer small, well-scoped routes under `src/routes/` and reusable logic under `src/services/`. Document new APIs in code comments so AI models (and humans) can follow the contracts easily.

---

**{{PROJECT\_DISPLAY\_NAME}}** — a stable, secure, **AI-ready** starting point for modern cloud web apps.
